hypotheses.title: Two-Phase Mechanoenhancer Activation Constitutes a Temporal Coincidence Gate
hypotheses.core_claim: Phase 1 (Piezo1→CaMKII→HDAC4/5→EP300→H3K27ac, <15 min) is a PREREQUISITE for Phase 2 (YAP→BRD4 condensate nucleation, 30-120 min) because BRD4 bromodomain requires H3K27ac marks for condensate anchoring. Piezo1 loss abolishes BOTH phases, not just Phase 1.
hypotheses.mechanistic_chain: ECM stiffness → PARALLEL: FAST LANE: Piezo1→Ca²⁺→CaMKII→HDAC4/5 export→EP300 freed→H3K27ac at mechanoenhancers (<15 min); SLOW LANE: stiffness→YAP nuclear (30-60 min)→YAP-BRD4 condensate→BUT: BRD4 BD1/BD2 requires H3K27ac marks for chromatin anchoring → marks from FAST LANE are nucleation sites for SLOW LANE

grounded_claims.claim: BRD4 bromodomain binds acetylated histones (H3K27ac, H3K14ac)
H3K27ac marks from Phase 1 are necessary for BRD4 condensate nucleation in mechanoenhancer context
grounded_claims.source: [INFERRED] — mechanism is logical but not directly tested

hypotheses.primary_prediction: Piezo1 KO reduces H3K27ac at mechanoenhancers at BOTH 15 min AND 120 min; Piezo1 KO reduces BRD4 CUT&RUN at mechanoenhancers at 120 min; YAP overexpression on soft ECM CANNOT drive BRD4 condensates without prior H3K27ac
hypotheses.discriminant: dCas9-p300 targeted to CYR61 mechanoenhancer in Piezo1 KO → rescue of BRD4 condensate nucleation proves H3K27ac marks are prerequisite

test_protocol.cell_system: Piezo1 KO (CRISPR) + control human lung fibroblasts
test_protocol.assay: CUT&RUN for H3K27ac, BRD4 at 0, 15, 30, 60, 120 min; STORM for BRD4 condensates
test_protocol.rescue: dCas9-p300 targeted to CYR61 mechanoenhancer in Piezo1 KO

hypotheses.novelty_vs_cycle1: H2 treats Phase 1 and Phase 2 as independent parallel pathways. C2-H1 proposes an obligatory dependency: Phase 1 H3K27ac marks are required for Phase 2 BRD4 condensate nucleation. This is a new causal claim.

hypotheses.title: Lamin A/C Concentration Sets the Cell-Intrinsic Stiffness-Sensing Threshold for Mechanoenhancer Activation
hypotheses.focus_area: H1 area (cell-context variation) — explains cell-to-cell heterogeneity
hypotheses.core_claim: On a uniform-stiffness substrate, cells with low lamin A/C (deformable nuclei) activate mechanoenhancer H3K27ac at lower ECM stiffness than cells with high lamin A/C (stiff nuclei). Lamin A/C acts as a cell-intrinsic rheostat calibrating the stiffness-sensing threshold.
hypotheses.mechanistic_chain: ECM stiffness → cytoskeletal tension → LINC complex (SUN1/SUN2) → nuclear deformation → two competing effects: low lamin A/C (deformable) → enhanced nuclear YAP enrichment → lower stiffness threshold for mechanoenhancer H3K27ac; high lamin A/C (stiff nucleus) → buffered deformation → higher threshold

grounded_claims.claim: Lamin A/C knockout → increased nuclear deformability → enhanced mechanosensing
grounded_claims.source: Multiple 2023-2025 papers; Swift et al. 2013 Science

grounded_claims.claim: Lamin A/C expression scales with ECM stiffness
grounded_claims.source: Multiple 2011-2025 papers (lamin A proportional to stiffness)

grounded_claims.claim: Cell-to-cell variability in nuclear YAP concentration on uniform substrates is observable
grounded_claims.source: Observable in published single-cell IF images

grounded_claims.claim: Lamin A/C variability → H3K27ac variability at mechanoenhancers in quantitatively predictable way

hypotheses.primary_prediction: scCUT&RUN for H3K27ac at CYR61 on 10 kPa shows bimodal distribution (ON/OFF cells); ON cells have lower lamin A/C by IF; lamin A overexpression shifts EC50 RIGHT (higher stiffness required); lamin A knockdown shifts EC50 LEFT

test_protocol.cell_system: Human lung fibroblasts + lamin A siRNA knockdown + lamin A OE lines
test_protocol.assay: scCUT&RUN for H3K27ac + quantitative IF for lamin A/C; threshold stiffness (EC50) vs lamin A/C level
test_protocol.positive_control: Progerin-expressing cells (aberrant lamin A, premature aging)

hypotheses.novelty_vs_cycle1: H1-H5 all treat the cell population as uniform. C2-H2 proposes cell-to-cell variability in lamin A/C is a systematic determinant of mechanoenhancer responsiveness — explaining why not all cells on stiff ECM activate mechanoenhancers equally.

hypotheses.title: Nuclear Actin Polymerization State Regulates MRTF-A Residence Time at CaRG-Box Mechanoenhancers
hypotheses.focus_area: H4 area (MRTF-A mechanism) — explains molecular basis of MRTF-A CaRG occupancy
hypotheses.core_claim: MRTF-A residence time at CaRG-box mechanoenhancers is regulated not only by cytoplasmic G-actin/MRTF-A nuclear entry, but by nuclear F-actin structures that tether MRTF-A via its RPEL domain to chromatin. Stiff ECM drives formin-mediated nuclear F-actin assembly → increased MRTF-A dwell time → EP300 recruitment above threshold → H3K27ac at CaRG-box mechanoenhancers.
hypotheses.mechanistic_chain: Stiff ECM → cytoskeletal tension → G-actin depletion → MRTF-A nuclear entry (classical) AND → mDia1/FHOD3 formin activity in nucleus → nuclear F-actin cables → MRTF-A RPEL domain contacts nuclear F-actin → MRTF-A dwell time at CaRG-box chromatin increases → EP300 dwell time increases → H3K27ac above threshold

grounded_claims.claim: Nuclear actin polymerization under mechanical stimulation and SRF activation
grounded_claims.source: Baarlink et al. 2013 Science + multiple reviews

grounded_claims.claim: mDia1 formin promotes nuclear actin polymerization
MRTF-A RPEL domain binds G-actin (classical mechanism)
Nuclear F-actin tethers MRTF-A at CaRG-box mechanoenhancers via RPEL domain
grounded_claims.source: [INFERRED] — RPEL/F-actin interaction in nuclear mechanoenhancer context not published

hypotheses.primary_prediction: SMIFH2 (formin inhibitor) on stiff ECM reduces MRTF-A CUT&RUN at CaRG-box mechanoenhancers despite equal nuclear MRTF-A concentration; MRTF-A-HaloTag SPT shows longer dwell time on stiff vs soft ECM at CaRG-box loci; Latrunculin A on stiff ECM (drives nuclear entry but prevents nuclear F-actin) reduces dwell time without changing nuclear MRTF-A level

test_protocol.cell_system: MRTF-A-HaloTag endogenous knock-in human lung fibroblasts
test_protocol.assay: Live SPT of MRTF-A-HaloTag (dwell time) + CUT&RUN for MRTF-A (steady-state) + LifeAct-GFP (nuclear F-actin)
test_protocol.focus_loci: CaRG-box mechanoenhancers vs TEAD-motif mechanoenhancers (internal control)

hypotheses.novelty_vs_cycle1: H4 predicts MRTF-A OCCUPIES CaRG-box mechanoenhancers. C2-H3 explains WHY (nuclear F-actin tether via RPEL) and makes a dwell-time prediction not captured in H4. Nuclear F-actin as a stiffness-dependent MRTF-A chromatin tether is a new mechanistic concept.

self_critique.cross_hypothesis_compatibility: C2-H1 × C2-H2 × C2-H3 are non-overlapping mechanistic classes: temporal dependency (C2-H1), cell-intrinsic threshold (C2-H2), MRTF-A dwell time (C2-H3). All three can be simultaneously true.
self_critique.novelty_vs_cycle1: All three are genuine cycle 2 advances — not refinements of H1-H5 but second-order consequences of cycle 1 mechanistic chains.
self_critique.groundedness_concern: C2-H2 has the highest inference ratio (lamin A/C → H3K27ac at mechanoenhancers). C2-H3's RPEL/F-actin interaction in nuclear context is inferred. C2-H1 is the most strongly grounded (BRD4 bromodomain mechanism is textbook).
